novel_features.name: Top-N finalist promotion
novel_features.description: Promote the top-N candidates from a list into a finalist sub-list ranked by combined score, price, and availability.
novel_features.rationale: Requires correlating brandability score, current price, and RDAP availability in a single local query — no API call returns all three.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to converge on a buyable shortlist, this collapses 'fetch scores, fetch prices, fetch availability, sort, persist' into one deterministic call.

novel_features.name: 5-year true-cost budget filter
novel_features.description: Filter candidates whose 5-year total cost (registration + 4 renewals) is under your ceiling, sorted ascending.
novel_features.rationale: Registrar UIs hide year-2 renewal prices until checkout; Porkbun's pricing endpoint is the only no-auth source that exposes both registration and renewal prices for every TLD.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Avoid the $12-year-1 / $4,800-renewal trap before falling in love with a name.

novel_features.name: Side-by-side compare
novel_features.description: One row per domain with score, length, TLD prestige, cross-registrar price, RDAP status, and drop flag.
novel_features.rationale: Joins every synced table by domain — no current CLI or web tool returns all dimensions in one structured response.
novel_features.example: domain-goat-pp-cli compare kindred.io kindred.ai kindred.studio --json --select status,price,score
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent or user finalize a shortlist in one command instead of cross-referencing 5 tabs.

novel_features.name: Drop timeline by score
novel_features.description: Time-axis view of every watched domain hitting pendingDelete/redemptionPeriod, filtered by brandability score and TLD.
novel_features.rationale: Reads persisted RDAP events_json — ExpiredDomains.net is web-only with no score filter; raw WHOIS output is unstructured text.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Drop-catchers and brand investors want to know 'what's queued for next week that's worth bidding on?' This answers it.

novel_features.name: Why-killed audit
novel_features.description: Show why a domain is no longer on the active shortlist: status, score, notes, tag history, last price.
novel_features.rationale: FTS5 over candidates.notes + tags joined with the last pricing/RDAP snapshot — recovers institutional memory weeks after the kill decision.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agency teams kill the same name twice in a sprint without this — and an agent re-suggesting a killed name burns user trust.

novel_features.name: Pricing arbitrage radar
novel_features.description: Rank TLDs by renewal-delta (year-1 trap risk) or by prestige-to-price ratio.
novel_features.rationale: Porkbun's public pricing endpoint is the only no-auth source publishing both registration and renewal prices for 600+ TLDs — aggregating them surfaces structural facts neither registrar UIs nor comparison sites show.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Helps users and agents avoid TLDs where year-2 pricing destroys the deal.

novel_features.name: Drop re-release window
novel_features.description: Compute the exact UTC re-release window for a domain in pendingDelete (RDAP event + 5-day grace).
novel_features.rationale: ICANN's expiry → redemption → pendingDelete → drop windows are deterministic but unsurfaced by any CLI — we read RDAP events_json and add the RFC grace.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Drop-catchers need minute-level timing; this replaces hand-parsed WHOIS regex.

novel_features.name: Seed-to-TLD affinity
novel_features.description: Given a seed keyword, rank TLDs by suffix-semantics fit, historical availability rate in local history, and price tier.
novel_features.rationale: Joins tlds × pricing × candidates from the local store; grounds 'should I look at .ai or .studio for this?' in cached data instead of gut feel.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Helps an agent pick generative TLDs before running expensive permutation passes.

narrative.headline: Identify domains worth buying — across RDAP, WHOIS, and Porkbun pricing — without ever leaving the terminal.
narrative.value_prop: domain-goat absorbs everything dnstwist, openrdap, whois, and the abandoned domainr-cli ever did, then transcends with a local SQLite shortlist that knows your scores, your notes, your watch list, and 5-year renewal cost for every TLD — so you can find domains worth registering instead of just typing names into instant-domain-search.com five at a time.
narrative.auth_narrative: No auth required for the headline path. RDAP, WHOIS port 43, and the Porkbun public pricing endpoint are all open. Optional registrar adapters (Namecheap, Porkbun authed, Name.com, Domainr) enrich pricing and suggestions when you set keys via `auth set-key`.

quickstart.comment: Pull the IANA RDAP bootstrap and Porkbun pricing snapshot once — everything offline after this.
RDAP-native availability with WHOIS fallback for the three TLDs you're stuck on.
Generate 50 brandable variants, filter to only available ones, all offline.

quickstart.command: domain-goat-pp-cli lists add ai-startup kindred.ai lumen.ai novella.ai
quickstart.comment: Save survivors to a shortlist with optional notes and tags.
Rank by combined score/price/availability and promote the top 10 to a finalist sub-list.
Side-by-side: scores, prices, RDAP status, drop flags.

troubleshoots.symptom: RDAP returns 5xx or no bootstrap entry for a ccTLD
troubleshoots.fix: domain-goat-pp-cli falls back to WHOIS port 43 automatically; verify with `--source rdap` vs `--source whois`.
GoDaddy gated their API in 2023; new keys require >=10 domains on the account. Use Porkbun or Name.com instead.

troubleshoots.symptom: Verisign WHOIS port 43 tarpitting your bulk run
troubleshoots.fix: Add `--prefer-rdap` (default) or `--parallel 2` to slow down; RDAP at rdap.verisign.com is rate-limit safer for .com/.net.
Domainr ships only via RapidAPI; run `domain-goat-pp-cli auth set-key domainr <RapidAPI-key>` and verify with `doctor`.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for domain-goat when you need to identify (not transact) domains worth registering — bulk-checking a name list across many TLDs, generating brandable variants, scoring candidates, watching for drops, comparing 5-year true cost across TLDs, or driving any of those workflows from an agent via MCP. Skip it if you already know which domain you want and just need to buy it — that's a registrar's job.

recipes.title: Friday brand pitch — generate 50 ranked candidates
recipes.explanation: Generate brandable variants, score each, filter by Porkbun renewal-price ceiling — agent-friendly JSON ready for the deck.

recipes.title: Persist a shortlist and promote the top 10
recipes.command: domain-goat-pp-cli lists add brand-sprint kindred.io lumen.ai novella.studio --tags founder
recipes.explanation: Save survivors to a candidate list; `shortlist promote --list brand-sprint --top 10` then picks finalists.

recipes.title: Drop-catch worth-bidding-on hunt
recipes.explanation: Two weeks of upcoming drops, score-filtered, agent-narrow output.

recipes.title: Five-year true-cost shortlist
recipes.explanation: Cuts the $12-year-1 / $4,800-renewal trap before you fall in love with a name.

recipes.title: Why did we kill this one?
recipes.explanation: FTS5 over notes + tags + last RDAP/pricing snapshot — recovers institutional memory weeks later.

recipes.title: Agent-friendly compare
recipes.explanation: Dotted-path --select keeps the agent's context tight — each row is ~80 bytes instead of the full RDAP envelope.

trigger_phrases: check if this domain is available
